column2: What is the main idea of cloud computing in Objective 4.2?
column4: Cloud computing provides scalable, on-demand computing resources through cloud-style infrastructure that can be provisioned and consumed as needed.
column5: Keep the answer at the concept level. This wording includes public, private, hybrid, and community cloud models without implying that every cloud must be externally provider-managed.
column9: CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 4.2; Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.47
column10: A+::220-1201::4.2 A+::220-1201::Domain4-VirtualizationCloud A+::220-1201::CloudComputingConcepts Basic Cloud Source::Messer-v170

column2: An organization runs a cloud environment restricted to its own users and workloads. Which cloud deployment model is this?
column5: The key clue is dedicated use by one organization. A private cloud can still use cloud-style automation and virtualization; it is not defined only by physical location.
column9: CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 4.2; Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 pp.47-48
column10: A+::220-1201::4.2 A+::220-1201::Domain4-VirtualizationCloud A+::220-1201::CloudComputingConcepts Basic HighYield Cloud PrivateCloud Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: A provider offers cloud services over the Internet to many unrelated customers. Which cloud deployment model is this?
column5: Public cloud focuses on broad provider access and shared provider infrastructure. It does not mean customer data is publicly visible.
column9: CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 4.2; Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 pp.47-48
column10: A+::220-1201::4.2 A+::220-1201::Domain4-VirtualizationCloud A+::220-1201::CloudComputingConcepts Basic HighYield Cloud PublicCloud Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: A company keeps sensitive internal systems in its private cloud but runs customer-facing web services in a public cloud. Which deployment model combines these environments?
column5: Hybrid cloud combines private and public cloud resources. The card tests the combination, not which workload belongs in each environment.
column9: CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 4.2; Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.47
column10: A+::220-1201::4.2 A+::220-1201::Domain4-VirtualizationCloud A+::220-1201::CloudComputingConcepts Basic HighYield Cloud HybridCloud Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: Several organizations with similar requirements share the same cloud environment. Which cloud deployment model best matches this?
column5: Community cloud is shared by a defined group of organizations. The trap is confusing "shared by a community" with public cloud, which is broadly available to many customers.
column9: CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 4.2; Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.47
column10: A+::220-1201::4.2 A+::220-1201::Domain4-VirtualizationCloud A+::220-1201::CloudComputingConcepts Basic HighYield Cloud CommunityCloud Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: A cloud provider supplies virtual servers, storage, and networking, while the customer manages the operating system and applications. Which service model is this?
column5: IaaS provides the infrastructure layer. The customer still has significant management responsibility above that layer.
column9: CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 4.2; Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.47
column10: A+::220-1201::4.2 A+::220-1201::Domain4-VirtualizationCloud A+::220-1201::CloudComputingConcepts Basic HighYield Cloud IaaS Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: A developer uses a cloud-hosted application framework and does not manage the underlying servers or operating systems. Which service model is this?
column5: PaaS sits between raw infrastructure and a finished application. The customer builds on the platform instead of managing the full server stack.
column9: CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 4.2; Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.47
column10: A+::220-1201::4.2 A+::220-1201::Domain4-VirtualizationCloud A+::220-1201::CloudComputingConcepts Basic HighYield Cloud PaaS Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: Users access a complete cloud-hosted application without installing or maintaining the application locally. Which service model is this?
column5: SaaS is the finished application model. The customer still manages things like user access and data choices, but not the application hosting stack.
column9: CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 4.2; Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.47
column10: A+::220-1201::4.2 A+::220-1201::Domain4-VirtualizationCloud A+::220-1201::CloudComputingConcepts Basic HighYield Cloud SaaS Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: In cloud computing, what is the difference between shared resources and dedicated resources?
column4: Shared resources are drawn from infrastructure used by multiple customers or workloads, while dedicated resources are reserved for one customer, organization, or workload.
column5: This distinction is about resource allocation, not whether the service is secure. Shared cloud platforms still use logical separation between tenants.
column9: CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 4.2; Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.48
column10: A+::220-1201::4.2 A+::220-1201::Domain4-VirtualizationCloud A+::220-1201::CloudComputingConcepts Basic Cloud Resources Source::Messer-v170

column2: A cloud service tracks CPU time, storage use, and data transfer so customers can be charged based on actual usage. Which cloud characteristic is this?
column5: Metering measures consumption. It can support billing, reporting, or chargeback, but it is not the same as automatically adding or removing resources.
column9: CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 4.2; Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.48
column10: A+::220-1201::4.2 A+::220-1201::Domain4-VirtualizationCloud A+::220-1201::CloudComputingConcepts Basic HighYield Cloud Metered Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: In cloud metering, what is the difference between ingress and egress traffic?
column4: Ingress traffic enters the cloud service; egress traffic leaves the cloud service.
column5: Cloud cost questions often care about traffic direction. Egress is especially worth recognizing because moving data out of a cloud service may be metered separately.
column9: CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 4.2; Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.48
column10: A+::220-1201::4.2 A+::220-1201::Domain4-VirtualizationCloud A+::220-1201::CloudComputingConcepts Basic HighYield Cloud Metered Networking Source::Messer-v170

column2: How does non-metered cloud utilization differ from metered utilization?
column4: Non-metered utilization uses a fixed block, allocation, or flat access model instead of charging directly for each measured unit of use.
column5: The official objective names metered utilization, and non-metered appears as a useful contrast in the approved notes. Keep this as a contrast card rather than expanding into provider pricing examples.
column9: CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 4.2; Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.48
column10: A+::220-1201::4.2 A+::220-1201::Domain4-VirtualizationCloud A+::220-1201::CloudComputingConcepts Basic Cloud Metered Source::Messer-v170

column2: A cloud application automatically increases resources during heavy demand and reduces them when demand drops. Which cloud characteristic is this?
column5: Elasticity is about scaling up and down with demand. It is not simply having redundant systems or a large initial capacity.
column9: CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 4.2; Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.48
column10: A+::220-1201::4.2 A+::220-1201::Domain4-VirtualizationCloud A+::220-1201::CloudComputingConcepts Basic HighYield Cloud Elasticity Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: A cloud service uses redundant systems in multiple locations so users can still access the service if one location has a problem. Which cloud characteristic is this?
column5: Availability focuses on keeping the service reachable. Redundancy can support availability, but redundancy itself is not the characteristic named in the objective.
column9: CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 4.2; Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.48
column10: A+::220-1201::4.2 A+::220-1201::Domain4-VirtualizationCloud A+::220-1201::CloudComputingConcepts Basic HighYield Cloud Availability Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: A cloud storage service keeps the same current files available across multiple locations and client devices. Which cloud characteristic is this?
column5: File synchronization keeps data consistent across places. It is not the same as elasticity, which changes resource capacity.
column9: CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 4.2; Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.48
column10: A+::220-1201::4.2 A+::220-1201::Domain4-VirtualizationCloud A+::220-1201::CloudComputingConcepts Basic Cloud Synchronization Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: What does multitenancy mean in cloud computing?
column4: Multiple customers or tenants use the same cloud infrastructure while their data and access remain logically separated.
column5: Multitenancy helps explain public cloud scale. It does not mean tenants can see or manage each other's data.
column9: CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 4.2; Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.48
column10: A+::220-1201::4.2 A+::220-1201::Domain4-VirtualizationCloud A+::220-1201::CloudComputingConcepts Basic HighYield Cloud Multitenancy Source::Messer-v170

column2: Across IaaS, PaaS, and SaaS, what changes as the provider manages more of the service stack?
column4: The customer has less responsibility for the underlying infrastructure and platform, while the provider takes over more management of the stack.
column5: This is the core service-model comparison. IaaS leaves more to the customer, PaaS manages the platform, and SaaS delivers a finished application.
column9: CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 4.2; Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.47
column10: A+::220-1201::4.2 A+::220-1201::Domain4-VirtualizationCloud A+::220-1201::CloudComputingConcepts Basic HighYield Cloud ServiceModels Source::Messer-v170

column2: A public cloud customer uses compute capacity from a large provider pool also used by other customers. Which resource characteristic is being used?
column5: The clue is provider capacity used by multiple customers. This card reinforces resource sharing without implying that tenant data is mixed.
column9: CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 4.2; Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.48
column10: A+::220-1201::4.2 A+::220-1201::Domain4-VirtualizationCloud A+::220-1201::CloudComputingConcepts Basic Cloud PublicCloud Resources Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: How can you distinguish elasticity from availability in a cloud scenario?
column4: Elasticity changes resource capacity as demand changes; availability keeps the service reachable when users need it.
column5: This is a common distractor split. Scaling for load points to elasticity; continuing service through failures points to availability.
column9: CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 4.2; Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.48
column10: A+::220-1201::4.2 A+::220-1201::Domain4-VirtualizationCloud A+::220-1201::CloudComputingConcepts Basic HighYield Cloud Elasticity Availability Source::Messer-v170
